generator_frequency.label: NPC Generation Frequency
generator_frequency.description: Consider generating new NPC concepts every N turns

demand_driven_generation.label: Story-Driven Character Creation
demand_driven_generation.description: Only create a new character when the story direction, an open question, or an active thread clearly calls for one -- each new character must cite the need it serves. Off restores the old behavior of speculatively keeping the unintroduced pool topped up.

introduction_enabled.label: Auto-Introduce NPCs
introduction_enabled.description: Let the Introduction Agent decide when to introduce new characters

max_unintroduced_pool.label: Max Unintroduced Pool
max_unintroduced_pool.description: Maximum NPCs kept in the unintroduced pool before generation stops

autonomous_travel_enabled.label: Enable Autonomous NPC Travel
autonomous_travel_enabled.description: Let unintroduced/cooled-down NPCs wander the world over time

travel_cooldown_turns.label: Travel Cooldown (turns)
travel_cooldown_turns.description: Turns since last contact before an introduced NPC can resume autonomous travel

travel_motivation_use_llm.label: LLM-Based Travel Motivation
travel_motivation_use_llm.description: Use an LLM to decide which NPCs are motivated to travel toward the player (richer but slower/costlier). When off, a fast rule-based heuristic is used.

present_character_context.label: Always Include Present Characters
present_character_context.description: Inject the profiles of characters currently in the scene (traveling with the player or at the player's location) directly into the storyteller's context every turn, independent of RAG retrieval.

scene_presence_use_llm.label: LLM Scene Presence Check
scene_presence_use_llm.description: In saves without location tracking, use a fast LLM call to decide which known characters are physically in the current scene (so they reach the storyteller's context). When off, characters count as present only when named in the recent story.

embed_profiles.label: Store Character Profiles in RAG
embed_profiles.description: When a character is introduced, embed their full profile (appearance, personality, role) into memory so it stays retrievable throughout the story.

capture_story_characters.label: Capture Story-Introduced Characters
capture_story_characters.description: Detect significant characters the storyteller introduces on its own (unnamed ones are tracked under a descriptive epithet until the story names them), generate full profiles for them, and track them as known NPCs.

track_character_changes.label: Auto-Track Character Changes
track_character_changes.description: After each turn, check the scene for lasting changes to the characters in it -- new names or revealed names, appearance changes, personality shifts, a changed pitch or narrative role, death or departure -- and update their records automatically.

mutation_schema.npc_introductions: array of objects: {npc_id: string, name: string (the character's name as mentioned in the story), first_impression: string (one sentence describing how they met the player), notes: string (optional evolving observations about the character)}
mutation_schema.npc_party_travel: array of objects: {npc_id: string, joining: boolean (true if the narrative shows this NPC joining the player as a traveling companion, false if it shows them leaving/parting ways with the player)}

command_help./npc: Manage a character: /npc generate [request] | /npc add <data> | /npc update <id> | /npc edit <id> <data> | /npc delete <id>
